plan.name: Package dependency audit
plan.recommendedAction: Inspect dependency and script changes, verify lockfile intent, run full install/build checks in a separate batch, then commit only package files if approved.

plan.name: Prisma migration/schema audit
plan.recommendedAction: Review seed and migration files, confirm schema intent, require backup evidence and explicit migration approval before any migration-related commit.
plan.commitPolicy: separate commit; no migration in audit mode

plan.name: Admin/API/core audit
plan.recommendedAction: Split by behavior domain, verify auth and data mutation boundaries, and run targeted tests before commit.

plan.name: Unknown file triage
plan.recommendedAction: Assign ownership and source for each group. Exclude generated artifacts and only commit intentional source/content changes.

plan.name: EBOS report residue commit
plan.recommendedAction: Review report-only diffs for date/status correctness and no secrets, then commit as a report-only batch if still needed.
plan.commitPolicy: dedicated report-only commit allowed after review

plan.name: Promotion Manager development branch preparation
plan.recommendedAction: Create or switch to a clean isolated worktree/branch only after remaining dirty ownership is understood, so new development does not mix with legacy residue.
plan.commitPolicy: start from clean or explicitly isolated state

guardrails: No git add during audit-only stages.
No git commit or push until the selected batch passes separate review.
No Prisma migration command without explicit migration approval phrase and backup evidence.
No server, Docker, Nginx, SSH, or deployment command during dirty audit stages.
Do not delete untracked files without explicit user confirmation.
recommendedOrder: EBOS report residue commit can be handled first because it is low risk and scoped.
Package and Prisma audits should be separate high-risk tracks.
Admin/API/core audits should be split by behavior and test surface.
Unknown/generated triage should run before Promotion Manager work starts.
